payload.text: generar un chat para hacer consultas en la información recogida en una hoja de google y pueda acceder a la informacion actualizada bbdd: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-3u_uuEcPW98KvqKQ_ivdW0qG9FA3YMoF-Cl2zfuZI0/edit?pli=1&gid=324358422#gid=324358422 . al usuario le debe de dar respuesta del estado de cada tienda, y hay dos periodos de consulta, anual y mensual, deberas analizar y detectar desviaciones comparado con nacional y región e indicar las prioridades de mejora segun los indicadores y objetivos marcados en nacional y regional

**Defining the Core Need**

I'm now zeroing in on the fundamental requirement: crafting a React chat interface that will engage with a Google Sheet, which will analyze store performance across annual and monthly periods, providing comparisons within the UI. I have begun to conceptualize the specific API interactions needed for data retrieval and analysis.


**Refining the Approach**

I'm now integrating regional and national benchmarks, alongside deviation detection to pinpoint improvement areas. I'm focusing on aligning these with specified objectives. I'm now setting up the React 18+ components for this, and starting to sketch out the required API interactions, leveraging the Gemini API.


**Prioritizing Data Access**

I'm now focusing on a reliable data acquisition strategy, given the Google Sheet URL. I'm prioritizing direct CSV fetching for real-time updates, but will need a fallback. A Data Context mechanism will handle potential CORS or authorization issues with user input. If direct fetch fails, I'll offer a UI for pasting data or confirming web publishing to ensure a seamless workflow.


**Structuring the API Interaction**

I'm now integrating the data context with the chosen Gemini model, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, treating fetched CSV data as a system instruction to analyze and answer queries. I'm focusing on UI/UX considerations, like dashboard charts and responsive design, all styled with Tailwind. The file structure is also taking shape.


**Refining the Query Handling**

I'm now establishing a robust approach for managing user queries within the system. I'm focusing on crafting a comprehensive system instruction for the Gemini model, incorporating the store, region, indicator (Sales, Traffic, Conversion, etc.) and period (Month/Year) dimensions. I'll need to handle diverse user inputs effectively. I am thinking of strategies for data summarization in cases where larger datasets exist. I am also working on the flow between the UI and the data context.


**Adapting to Gemini Output**

I'm now integrating the Gemini model to parse results. My main focus remains on text-based analysis, but I'll add the bonus ability to render charts if Gemini returns structured JSON, to respond to more complex requests. This will ensure flexibility.

\n\n

**Structuring the Data Analysis**

I've refocused on refining the prompt to analyze deviations and comparisons, implying the CSV likely includes "National" and "Regional" rows. I am preparing for the AI to make those calculations if they don't explicitly exist, for greater flexibility with user data. I am working on the system instructions for Gemini.


**Implementing Data Fetching**

I'm now implementing the Google Sheet data fetching, prioritizing the direct URL approach and adding a fallback mechanism with a text area for data input in case of failures. The UI will initially use mock data for a seamless demo experience. I've begun integrating the Gemini API, utilizing `gemini-3-flash-preview` and setting up the core chat interface components with message rendering, and managing the state of the API key within `App.tsx`, along with data loading. I'm focusing on user experience, and plan to include a data preview panel.


**Designing the Interface**

I am now focusing on the React component structure. I'll include a sidebar for settings and data status, and the main chat interface. I'm prioritizing direct CSV fetching to analyze annual/monthly data, deviations and priorities, with GID `324358422` in mind. I'm focusing on text-based results initially, but Recharts may provide visuals when analysis comparison is implied. To ensure seamless workflow, I will offer a manual input method or sample dataset if direct fetch fails. I plan to use Markdown to render the text results.


**Assembling the Core Components**

I'm now focusing on the component imports, ensuring they are light and efficient. I have decided to handle text formatting from Gemini responses with a simple approach, avoiding external Markdown libraries where I can. The system prompt for Gemini is now finalized; it's designed to compare store metrics against national and regional averages, or calculate them, highlighting negative deviations as improvement priorities.


**Clarifying Data Types**

I am now focusing on how the system will distinguish between 'Annual' (Year to Date) and 'Monthly' (Current Month) data from the Google Sheet. I am now opting to handle this directly in the AI prompt, and I'm self-correcting my use of `react-markdown` and will implement a message bubble component instead. I am also making some small code changes to the Gemini service.

entries.description: Service to handle Google Sheet CSV fetching
